# Tactical Techniques

**Technique 1: Gegenpressing. Win the ball back within 5 seconds of losing it.**

**Technique 2: Full-Backs as Creators. Klopp's full-backs (Robertson, TAA) create chances.**

**Technique 3: Front Three Rotation. Mane, Firmino, Salah interchanged positions constantly.**

**Case: The Trent Role. Alexander-Arnold became the Premier League's most creative defender under Klopp.**

**Case: The False 9. Firmino played as a false 9, dropping deep to create space for the wingers.**

**Case: The High Line. Klopp's teams defend high, compressing the pitch.**

**Case: The Set Piece. Klopp hired set-piece specialists. Liverpool became lethal from corners.**
**Case: The Pressing Trap. Klopp's teams trap opponents in specific zones of the pitch.**
**Case: The Transition. Klopp's teams are most dangerous in transition — immediately after winning the ball.**
**Case: The Adaptation. Klopp adjusted his tactics for different opponents. He was not a one-trick pony.**
**Case: The Substitution. Klopp's substitutions often changed games. He read matches well.**
**Case: The Half-Time Talk. Klopp was famous for his half-time team talks that turned matches around.**
**Case: The Pressing Triggers. Klopp's teams press when specific triggers appear — a bad pass, a heavy touch, a backward pass.**
**Case: The Full-Back Role. Klopp revolutionized the full-back position. Robertson and TAA became creators.**
